conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That sounds challenging. Can you tell me more about your job as a librarian? What's been the most stressful part for you?

Well, it's just that I'm always trying to get everything perfect, and if I don't meet those deadlines, it reflects poorly on me, right? And to be honest, I've been having trouble concentrating lately too. I mean, I'll be in the middle of a task and suddenly remember something else I need to do, so I switch over and try to tackle that instead.
I see. So you're experiencing some trouble with focus. How has your sleep been lately?
Honestly? It's been pretty rough. I've been lying awake for hours, thinking about all the things I need to get done tomorrow, and then I'll finally drift off just as my alarm is going off.
That sounds like it would be frustrating. Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or energy levels?

Yeah... actually, my appetite has been pretty minimal lately. I mean, sometimes I'll skip lunch because I'm too stressed to take a break and eat, and then I'll overdo it at dinner because I'm starving by that point.
That makes sense. How about your energy levels? Have you noticed any changes?

It's like... I used to be able to power through a long day of work without feeling drained, but now I feel exhausted even after just a few hours. Like today, for instance, I had to rush out the door and didn't have time for breakfast, so by mid-morning I was already feeling wiped.
Okay. Have you noticed any guilt or self-criticism about your situation?

Oh, definitely. I feel like I should be able to handle my workload better, but instead I'm constantly worried about not meeting deadlines and letting people down. It's like this nagging voice in the back of my head telling me I'm failing.
That can be really tough. What do you think is the most stressful part of your job?

I guess it's just the constant pressure to perform, you know? Like, if I don't meet those deadlines, people will start looking for someone else to take my place. And then there's this whole anxiety thing... honestly, sometimes I feel like I'm drowning in worries.
It sounds like you're shouldering a lot of responsibility. What do you think you would like to change about your job or daily routine?
Hmm... well, maybe if I could just get my energy levels up and be able to focus better, that would help a ton. And maybe setting aside some dedicated time for self-care wouldn't hurt either.
That sounds like a great idea. Do you have any hobbies or activities outside of work that bring you joy?

Actually, I used to love reading in my free time, but lately it's been tough finding the energy and focus to really get into a book. And then there's this one friend who's always on my case about not doing enough social stuff... honestly, sometimes it feels like everyone wants something from me.
It sounds like you're feeling overwhelmed by responsibilities both at work and in your personal life. How do you take care of yourself outside of work?

Honestly? Not very well lately. I mean, I used to love taking long walks on the weekends, but now that's just too exhausting for me. And then there are these guilt trips where I beat myself up over not doing enough... it's like this self-fulfilling prophecy.
It sounds like you're struggling with some self-criticism and guilt. What do you think your goals are right now? Are there any areas in your life where you'd like to see positive change?

Well, I guess my main goal is just to get a handle on this anxiety thing... maybe find some strategies for managing stress better. And honestly, it would be great if I could start sleeping through the night without waking up every hour.
That sounds like a great place to start. Is there anything specific that's been stressing you out recently?

Yeah, just general work-related stuff... feeling overwhelmed and behind on everything. But to be honest, sometimes it feels like my emotions are all over the place too. Like one minute I'm fine, and then the next I'm bursting into tears.
That sounds really tough. How do you usually handle those emotional moments?
Honestly? Not very well... I just kind of try to push through or distract myself until it passes. But sometimes that doesn't work, and I end up feeling even more overwhelmed.
It sounds like you're experiencing some emotional turmoil. Is there anything you think would help you cope with those feelings better?
Maybe if I could figure out what's causing all these emotions... I mean, sometimes it feels like my thoughts are just racing and I'm not really sure where they're coming from.
That can be really frustrating. Do you feel like there's anything in your life that you're putting off or avoiding?
Yeah... actually, I've been putting off some personal stuff for a while now. Like, I really need to call my mom and catch up, but it feels like too much effort right now.
It sounds like there are some things you're not addressing that might be contributing to your stress. Is there anything specific that's been causing those feelings of guilt lately?
Honestly? Just feeling like I'm not doing enough... for everyone, really. Like, my family expects me to be more social, and my boss wants me to take on more responsibilities at work.
That can be really overwhelming. What do you think would help you feel more grounded or centered?
Maybe if I could just get a sense of control over my life... like, feeling like I'm not spinning out of control all the time.
It sounds like you're seeking a sense of stability and control. Is there anything you've been looking forward to lately that might help you feel more positive?

Actually, yes! My friend is getting married soon, and I'm really excited to celebrate with her. And then there's this one book I've been wanting to read for ages... maybe I can find some time to get back into reading.
That sounds like something to look forward to. Before we wrap up today, are there any questions or concerns you have?
Yeah, actually... what do you think would be the most helpful thing for me to work on in the next few weeks?

Well, based on our conversation, I think it might be helpful for you to explore some strategies for managing your anxiety and stress. Maybe we can discuss some coping mechanisms or relaxation techniques that could help.
Yeah, that sounds good... thank you!
